summary:
Goldman (3): Largest US Tech net selling in 10+ years (-4.0 z-score) alongside remarkably strong overall US equity inflows signals levered US-exceptionalism positioning; rising stock dispersion puts premium on stock selection.

Apollo/Slok (3): Trade policy uncertainty declining + Strait of Hormuz reopening framed as bullish for business planning and hiring; AI ROI runway seen as long outside tech; profit margins remain constrained beyond tech sector.

DataTrek (1): First time in 2026 consensus earnings + historically average PE ratio justify 8% S&P 500 upside from current levels.

JPM/JPMAM (1): Employment advantage for recent college graduates has turned negative — a trend that began well before ChatGPT, per JPMAM data.

Bank counts: Goldman 3, Apollo/Slok 3, DataTrek 1, JPM 1.

banks.theme: Slok — policy clarity + Hormuz reopening framed bullish; AI ROI long outside tech; profit margins still subdued outside tech

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"Trade policy uncertainty continues to decline, and the Strait of Hormuz is reopening ... This is all bullish for business planning and hiring."

-Apollo Slok https://t.co/SRy63sRiL1

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
AI: The ROI Runway Could Be Long Outside the Tech Sector

Torsten at Apollo https://t.co/bJnBTnbusc

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: Torsten at Apollo: no signs of profit margins rising outside the tech sector https://t.co/JxcUkbiEFO

banks.theme: Goldman — record US Tech net selling at 10-yr high; strong overall US equity inflows; individual stock stories replacing index-level trading

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"U.S. equities no longer trading as one index; they're trading as collection of individual stories, raising importance of stock selection/creating much richer backdrop for dispersion/single-stock volatility through earnings"

Goldman Sachs via @LizAnnSonders https://t.co/1IRfw1vssV

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
Last "week's net selling in US Info Tech - in both $ and % terms - was the largest in more than 10 years (-4.0 z score)."

-GS Stankard https://t.co/O0OP1cVYLS

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"Flows into US equities have been remarkably strong so far... More signs of levered US exceptionalism positioning"

Goldman Sachs via @MikeZaccardi https://t.co/fLslSpSIUM

banks.theme: DataTrek — S&P 500 justified at 8% upside using consensus earnings + average PE, first time in 2026

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"For the first time this year, one can justify a bullish view on the S&P 500 using simply consensus earnings estimates and a historically average PE ratio. Against these modest assumptions, the index has 8% upside from here."

@DataTrekMB https://t.co/Xco1MRpAZ9
